preamble: The shift from the alveolar fricative [s] to the voiceless interdental fricative [θ] is a well-documented process of interdentalization. It is most famously associated with the 'ceceo' dialects of Southern Spain, where the distinction between historical sibilants was lost in favor of the interdental articulation.

examples.note: Ceceo: the systematic use of the interdental fricative for all sibilants.

sources: Penny: A History of the Spanish Language
